speaker: Christina
text: I'm fucking losing it.

speaker: Christina
text: I-I've got to clean up in here.

speaker: Narrator
text: One look at the assorted piles of rubbish dissuades Christina from that idea. Instead, she bargains that a focused attack on her project will relieve most of the stress weighing down on her. She turns on the livestream.

speaker: Narrator
text: Pasaya's just started, and as always, she's pontificating on the nature of things in our reality. There was a traditional belief amongst the 'hive' that this was her way of allowing fans to prepare themselves in lobbies ahead of time for her multiplayer sessions. That or the other theory that she had a bad habit of switching her chosen game at the last minute. Either way, Christina didn't have it in her to waste an evening on a vain chance to game with Pasaya. Even if she really, really wanted to.

speaker: Pasaya
text: Okay, everyone can see this?

speaker: Pasaya
text: I'm not worried about the claim, ya'll save these shits anyway, just upload it on one of those channels.

speaker: Pasaya
text: This is precisely the point where they ran out of manga-

speaker: Pasaya
text: I know the dude was a freak, I'm not saying you should give that guy any of your money.

speaker: Narrator
text: She thumbs through more of Blanche's notes. Copying some of the most useful and least legible parts of the data onto her own documents. Through this, she uncovered more interesting details about the parasite afflicting the fish.

speaker: Pasaya
text: I'm apologizing in advance, my chat-drones.

speaker: Pasaya
text: For the first few hours today I am locking in on a...

speaker: Pasaya
text: Fighting game.

speaker: Pasaya
text: If you want me to beat you in any way, you're gonna hafta get ready now..

speaker: Narrator
text: Blanche reiterates at several points that the creature's nervous system is sophisticated in a way that resembles the most advanced cephalopods. Further muddying the waters of what exactly this thing is, she notes that the creature's main form of survival involves...

speaker: Christina
text: Attaching itself to the brain…

speaker: Christina
text: Dangling its long body down the length of the fish and using its tentacles to intertwine itself with the organs of the host creature.

speaker: Narrator
text: At the end of its body is what counts as a second mouth capable of taking nutrients from or sharing with the host body. It's capable of expanding and contracting itself to allow for easier digestion of the host's food.

speaker: Christina
text: Other researchers in previous studies have noted that in warm periods these worms are unable to bond with a variety of species...

speaker: Narrator
text: Fish, bird, or mammal with little distinction. She further notes that…:

speaker: Blanche
text: This shit was so fucking disgusting, I couldn't bring myself to photograph it.

speaker: Blanche
text: One fish's entire digestive system had been bypassed by the parasite.

speaker: Blanche
text: The first sign of this is clear signs of...

speaker: Blanche
text: When I pried it out of the fish's corpse, I had incidentally removed the brain and spine with surgical precision.

speaker: Blanche
text: There were signs that the creature's tentacles had ingratiated themselves deep within the puny rain of the roach.

speaker: Blanche
text: Particularly in the areas controlling..

speaker: Christina
text: Sensory information.

speaker: Christina
text: Spatial awareness.

speaker: Christina
text: Sex.

speaker: Narrator
text: Like a switch being flipped in her mind, Christina was jolted by a sharp pain in her stomach. Pulling her down out of her grease-stained chair. Stewing on the floor in pain.
